name: Early Detection of Organ Failure [Minimal]
description: Focuses on developing and deploying machine learning models that predict organ failure using time series data for clinical evaluation.
justification:
This task demonstrates development and deployment of machine learning models predicting organ failure from ICU time series data. The use case focuses on **multi-organ failure prediction**, based on the premise that early detection enables reversible interventions, improving health outcomes and clinical workflow.

- **T6.2.1** — Select organ systems using MIMIC-EU data and outcome information, applying ELSI framework procedures developed by experts for federated data analysis access.
- **T6.2.2** — Establish a data adjudication committee for quality control and harmonized annotation of health outcomes, including organ failure in kidney, brain, liver, and cardiovascular systems.
- **T6.2.3** — Develop, test, and validate machine learning models predicting organ failure, accounting for regional differences, sex differences, and minorities.
- **T6.2.4** — Design clinical evaluation studies for machine learning systems.
- **T6.2.5** — Deploy machine learning systems for clinical evaluation at participating institutions.

Led by **Alexandre Mebazaa** at AP-HP, Assistance Publique – Hôpitaux de Paris.
